LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SOPHIE GASPARATOS, AND, STEPHEN LAWRENCE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST, 6TH, 7TH, 8TH, AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD THEY HAVE VACATED THE PREMISES TO SUPPORT THE 1ST, 8TH, AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS UNCERTAIN IN THAT IT CONTAINS MULTIPLE CLAIMS FOR RELIEF IN VIOLATION OF CRC 2.112. PLAINTIFFS MUST PLEAD THAT MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC ARE LIKELY TO BE DECEIVED BY DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS TO SUPPORT THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS BARRED BY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BECAUSE IT TURNS ON FACTS RESOLVED IN THAT AGREEMENT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, 5TH, AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
